content:
Net-new story gap table (continued — rows 19–36)

Story Gap Likelihood PM lens QA lens Dev lens Verdict Suggested missing BDD (Given/When/Then)
HRREC-91995
PH: Recruiter gets discard popup by changing pages		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User Story *As a* Recruiter, *I want* an explicit discard confirmation dialog to appear if I navigate away from the page after editing an email, *So that* I do not silently lose my unsent text h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Navigation with unsent text prompts confirmation *Given* I have entered text or made edits in the email rich text editor *When* I attempt to navigate away from the candidate profile or close th…
  • Salomon (Knowledge) — Message Builder admin flows (template dashboard, notification categories, email channel + notification templates) gate how recruiting email bodies and headers behave—preview vs send tooling differs by channel.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under <strong>Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting</strong> plus security on <strong>Candidate Communication</strong> and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon (Jira index) — Epic-level Salomon Jira query (two-way email) returned keys including HRREC-91946, HRREC-89765, HRREC-91707, HRREC-91706, HRREC-87325, HRREC-79876, HRREC-90136, HRREC-90926, HRREC-51359, HRREC-43605; use only lines relevant to HRREC-91995’s summary when refining.
  • Salomon (Slack) — No archived Slack hits returned for simple_query_string "HRREC-82977" OR "two-way email" recruiting (Salomon ES this run).
  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-91995, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“PH: Recr…
  • Peanut —Not queried — no per-row trigger in this batched HTML emit pass.
  • Finding: HRREC-91995: Jira scenarios sketch the happy path; lenses still flag negative paths and tenant configuration to confirm before sizing.
  • Recommended next step: In the next refinement, pick one failure or permission edge from this summary and ask engineering for the expected recruiter-visible behaviour.

Scenario: Negative path clarity — HRREC-91995

Given a recruiter authenticated for Conversational Email opens this task on a sandbox candidate profile with realistic tenant toggles
When the situation implied by this slice is stressed: PH: Recruiter gets discard popup by changing pages
Then the recruiter sees deterministic messaging and controls (no silent failure) consistent with this story’s Jira Scenarios

HRREC-91996
PH: Recruiter gets discard popup when they click the Discard button		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User Story *As a* Recruiter, *I want* an explicit confirmation popup if I attempt to discard a message I am actively composing, *So that* I avoid accidentally losing my unsent text h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Confirmation shown when editor contains unsent text *Given* I have entered text or made edits in the rich text editor *When* I click the "Discard" button *Then* I am presented with a confirmation popup war…
  • Salomon (Knowledge) — Internal Slack context: conversational SMS content lives on the conversation in the panel; outbound SMS notification reports differ; mobile vs desktop messaging parity is not guaranteed—use as channel-pattern reminder only for email UX promises.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under <strong>Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting</strong> plus security on <strong>Candidate Communication</strong> and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon (Jira index) — Epic-level Salomon Jira query (two-way email) returned keys including HRREC-91946, HRREC-89765, HRREC-91707, HRREC-91706, HRREC-87325, HRREC-79876, HRREC-90136, HRREC-90926, HRREC-51359, HRREC-43605; use only lines relevant to HRREC-91996’s summary when refining.
  • Salomon (Slack) — No archived Slack hits returned for simple_query_string "HRREC-82977" OR "two-way email" recruiting (Salomon ES this run).
  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-91996, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“PH: Recr…
  • Peanut —Not queried — no per-row trigger in this batched HTML emit pass.
  • Finding: HRREC-91996: Jira scenarios sketch the happy path; lenses still flag negative paths and tenant configuration to confirm before sizing.
  • Recommended next step: In the next refinement, pick one failure or permission edge from this summary and ask engineering for the expected recruiter-visible behaviour.

Scenario: Negative path clarity — HRREC-91996

Given a recruiter authenticated for Conversational Email opens this task on a sandbox candidate profile with realistic tenant toggles

Then the recruiter sees deterministic messaging and controls (no silent failure) consistent with this story’s Jira Scenarios

HRREC-91997
PH: Recruiter sees list of messages with unread ones marked in blue		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User story *As a* recruiter *I want* a thread list with unread markers in blue *So that* I triage volume quickly (transcript: unread in blue; list + tags discussion) h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Unread inbound highlights *Given* a thread has unread inbound messages *When* I view the list *Then* unread markers appear in blue per design h3. Scenario 2: Mark read on open elsewhere *Given* another recruiter opened t…

  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-84407: email read semantics differ from WhatsApp; state what you promise in the blue unread treatment versus what delivery or open receipts actually support.

  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-91997, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“PH: Recr…

No additional BDD suggested beyond Jira’s styling scenario for this pass.

HRREC-92004
PH: Recruiter sees error when sending message (REST error)		HighYellow

Material failure gap; one BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User Story *As a* Recruiter, *I want* structured UI error handling when my email fails to send due to a REST API error, *So that* I understand why the send was blocked and what manual actions I need to take to resolve the issue. h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Mid-session state change blocks send (Opt-out or Security Loss) *Given* I am actively drafting a message in the sliding side panel *When* I click "Send" but …

  • Peanut —Not queried — no per-row trigger in this batched HTML emit pass.
  • Finding: REST and network failures during send can lose recruiter trust if drafts and retry paths are ambiguous.
  • Recommended next step: Align with engineering on idempotent send handling and whether drafts survive REST errors before QA hardens suites.

Scenario: REST error recover-forward

Given a recruiter composes a valid email on an active thread
When the first send attempt returns the REST error class described in Jira
Then the thread and composer show the agreed error, preserve or discard drafts per policy, and allow a safe retry path

HRREC-92005
PH: Recruiter sees info message and cannot send email if the candidate changes their email		HighYellow

Identity change gap; one BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User Story *As a* Recruiter, *I want to* be blocked from sending new messages and see a clear explanation if a candidate's primary home email address changes mid-thread, *So that* I do not accidentally send communications to an outdated or incorrect address, ensuring data privacy and accuracy. h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Candidate email change hides actions and displays an info message *Given* an active 2-way e…

  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under <strong>Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting</strong> plus security on <strong>Candidate Communication</strong> and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-90306 / merge identity: primary email change plus merge should not leave recruiters replying as the wrong person or reading a thread keyed to a stale address.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon (Jira index) — Epic-level Salomon Jira query (two-way email) returned keys including HRREC-91946, HRREC-89765, HRREC-91707, HRREC-91706, HRREC-87325, HRREC-79876, HRREC-90136, HRREC-90926, HRREC-51359, HRREC-43605; use only lines relevant to HRREC-92005’s summary when refining.

  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-92005, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“PH: Recr…
  • Peanut —Not queried — no per-row trigger in this batched HTML emit pass.
  • Finding: Primary home email changes mid-thread can strand recruiters unless info banners and read-only history match policy.
  • Recommended next step: Workshop merge plus email-change sequencing with engineering so pause rules match SMS precedents where intended.

Scenario: Candidate email change mid-thread

Given a recruiter has an active two-way email thread open on the candidate profile
When the candidate’s primary home email changes while the panel is still open
Then Reply and Add hide with the info copy from Jira and historical messages remain read-only without silent resend targets
